---
title: "The Welfare Effect of Subsidies: A Case Study of Public Transport"
date: 2023-10-01
author:
  - name: Jorge Luis Ochoa Rincón
    url: https://jorgeluis8ar.github.io/
    orcid: 0009-0004-4113-8724
    affiliation: Department of Economics, Brown University
categories:
  - Development economics
  - Urban economics
  - Transport
  - Welfare
  - Public policy
pub-info:
  reference: >-
    <strong>Jorge Luis Ochoa Rincón</strong>, “The Welfare Effect of Subsidies: A Case Study of Public Transport” <em>Documentos CEDE</em> (2023)
  links:
    - name: English PDF
      url: /files/WP-Welfare-Effects-Subsidies-Case-Study-Public-Transport.pdf
      icon: bi bi-file-earmark-pdf
    - name: Spanish PDF
      url: /files/dcede2023-30.pdf
      icon: bi bi-file-earmark-pdf
    - name: SSRN / DOI
      url: https://dx.doi.org/10.2139/ssrn.4575115
      icon: bi bi-link-45deg
---

## Important links

- [English paper](/files/WP-Welfare-Effects-Subsidies-Case-Study-Public-Transport.pdf)
- [Spanish paper](/files/dcede2023-30.pdf)
- [SSRN / DOI](https://dx.doi.org/10.2139/ssrn.4575115)

## Abstract

This paper estimates welfare gains using detailed data from a public transport subsidy experiment in Bogotá, Colombia. The effective experimental variation in subsidy allocation allows the identification of key parameters in a discrete choice model, resulting in credible estimates of welfare gains for the beneficiaries of the intervention.

The program increased participants’ average monthly welfare, measured as consumer surplus, while the magnitude of those gains differed meaningfully across trip purposes and travel-time constraints. The project sits at the intersection of development economics, urban economics, and transport policy, and it continues to shape my broader research agenda on mobility and access.
